Ccsne tutorial1

In [1]:
Copied! 
import simple

import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
import simple import logging logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)

Describe isolist

In [2]:
Copied! 
isolist = {
        'H-1': 'H-1',
        'He-4': 'He-4',
        'C-12': 'C-12',
        'O-16': 'O-16',
        'Ne-20': 'Ne-20',
        'Si-28': 'Si-28',
        'Al-26': 'Al-26',
        'S-32': 'S-32',
        'Mg-24': 'Mg-24',
        'N-14': 'N-14',
        'K-40': 'K-40',
        'Ni-56': 'Ni-56',
        'Ti-46': 'Ti-46',
        'Ti-46*': 'Ti-46,Sc-46',
        'Ti-47': 'Ti-47',
        'Ti-47*': 'Ti-47,V-47,Sc-47,Ca-47,K-47',
        'Ti-48': 'Ti-48',
        'Ti-48*': 'Ti-48,V-48,Cr-48,Sc-48',
        'Ti-49': 'Ti-49',
        'Ti-49*': 'Ti-49,V-49,Cr-49,Sc-49,Ca-49,K-49',
        'Ti-50': 'Ti-50',
        'Ti-50*': 'Ti-50,Sc-50,Ca-50',
        'Fe-54':  'Fe-54',
        'Fe-54*': 'Fe-54,Co-54',
        'Fe-56': 'Fe-56',
        'Fe-56*': 'Fe-56,Ni-56,Co-56,Mn-56,Cr-56',
        'Fe-57': 'Fe-57',
        'Fe-57*': 'Fe-57,Ni-57,Co-57,Mn-57,Cr-57',
        'Fe-58': 'Fe-58',
        'Fe-58*': 'Fe-58,Co-58,Mn-58,Cr-58',
        'Ni-58':  'Ni-58',
        'Ni-58*': 'Ni-58,Cu-58',
        'Ni-60':  'Ni-60',
        'Ni-60*': 'Ni-60,Cu-60,Zn-60,Co-60,Fe-60',
        'Ni-61':  'Ni-61',
        'Ni-61*': 'Ni-61,Cu-61,Zn-61,Co-61,Fe-61',
        'Ni-62': 'Ni-62',
        'Ni-62*': 'Ni-62,Cu-62,Zn-62,Co-62,Fe-62',
        'Ni-64': 'Ni-64',
        'Ni-64*': 'Ni-64,Cu-64',
        'Zn-64': 'Zn-64',
        'Zn-64*': 'Zn-64,Ga-64,Ge-64,As-64,Se-64',
        'Zn-66': 'Zn-66',
        'Zn-66*': 'Zn-66,Ga-66,Ge-66,As-66,Se-66,Br-66,Cu-66,Ni-66,Co-66,Fe-66,Mn-66,Cr-66,V-66',
        'Zn-67': 'Zn-67',
        'Zn-67*': 'Zn-67,Ga-67,Ge-67,As-67,Se-67,Br-67,Cu-67,Ni-67,Co-67,Fe-67,Mn-67,Cr-67,V-67',
        'Zn-68': 'Zn-68',
        'Zn-68*': 'Zn-68,Ga-68,Ge-68,As-68,Se-68,Br-68,Cu-68,Ni-68,Co-68,Fe-68,Mn-68,Cr-68',
        'Zn-70': 'Zn-70',
        'Zn-70*': 'Zn-70,Ga-70,Ge-70,As-70,Se-70,Br-70,Cu-70,Ni-70,Co-70,Fe-70,Mn-70,Cr-70',
}
isolist = { 'H-1': 'H-1', 'He-4': 'He-4', 'C-12': 'C-12', 'O-16': 'O-16', 'Ne-20': 'Ne-20', 'Si-28': 'Si-28', 'Al-26': 'Al-26', 'S-32': 'S-32', 'Mg-24': 'Mg-24', 'N-14': 'N-14', 'K-40': 'K-40', 'Ni-56': 'Ni-56', 'Ti-46': 'Ti-46', 'Ti-46*': 'Ti-46,Sc-46', 'Ti-47': 'Ti-47', 'Ti-47*': 'Ti-47,V-47,Sc-47,Ca-47,K-47', 'Ti-48': 'Ti-48', 'Ti-48*': 'Ti-48,V-48,Cr-48,Sc-48', 'Ti-49': 'Ti-49', 'Ti-49*': 'Ti-49,V-49,Cr-49,Sc-49,Ca-49,K-49', 'Ti-50': 'Ti-50', 'Ti-50*': 'Ti-50,Sc-50,Ca-50', 'Fe-54': 'Fe-54', 'Fe-54*': 'Fe-54,Co-54', 'Fe-56': 'Fe-56', 'Fe-56*': 'Fe-56,Ni-56,Co-56,Mn-56,Cr-56', 'Fe-57': 'Fe-57', 'Fe-57*': 'Fe-57,Ni-57,Co-57,Mn-57,Cr-57', 'Fe-58': 'Fe-58', 'Fe-58*': 'Fe-58,Co-58,Mn-58,Cr-58', 'Ni-58': 'Ni-58', 'Ni-58*': 'Ni-58,Cu-58', 'Ni-60': 'Ni-60', 'Ni-60*': 'Ni-60,Cu-60,Zn-60,Co-60,Fe-60', 'Ni-61': 'Ni-61', 'Ni-61*': 'Ni-61,Cu-61,Zn-61,Co-61,Fe-61', 'Ni-62': 'Ni-62', 'Ni-62*': 'Ni-62,Cu-62,Zn-62,Co-62,Fe-62', 'Ni-64': 'Ni-64', 'Ni-64*': 'Ni-64,Cu-64', 'Zn-64': 'Zn-64', 'Zn-64*': 'Zn-64,Ga-64,Ge-64,As-64,Se-64', 'Zn-66': 'Zn-66', 'Zn-66*': 'Zn-66,Ga-66,Ge-66,As-66,Se-66,Br-66,Cu-66,Ni-66,Co-66,Fe-66,Mn-66,Cr-66,V-66', 'Zn-67': 'Zn-67', 'Zn-67*': 'Zn-67,Ga-67,Ge-67,As-67,Se-67,Br-67,Cu-67,Ni-67,Co-67,Fe-67,Mn-67,Cr-67,V-67', 'Zn-68': 'Zn-68', 'Zn-68*': 'Zn-68,Ga-68,Ge-68,As-68,Se-68,Br-68,Cu-68,Ni-68,Co-68,Fe-68,Mn-68,Cr-68', 'Zn-70': 'Zn-70', 'Zn-70*': 'Zn-70,Ga-70,Ge-70,As-70,Se-70,Br-70,Cu-70,Ni-70,Co-70,Fe-70,Mn-70,Cr-70', }

What happens here

In [3]:
Copied! 
models = simple.load_collection('TiFeNiZn.hdf5','../data/SIMPLE_CCSNe_v2.hdf5', default_isolist=isolist, overwrite=True)
models = simple.load_collection('TiFeNiZn.hdf5','../data/SIMPLE_CCSNe_v2.hdf5', default_isolist=isolist, overwrite=True)
INFO:SIMPLE.models:Creating: "TiFeNiZn.hdf5" from database: "../data/SIMPLE_CCSNe_v2.hdf5"

In [4]:
Copied! 
models[0].onion_lbounds
models[0].onion_lbounds
Out[4]:
array([(1649, 1458, 1081, 910, 910, 184, 95, 0)],
      dtype=[('H', '<i8'), ('He/N', '<i8'), ('He/C', '<i8'), ('O/C', '<i8'), ('O/Ne', '<i8'), ('O/Si', '<i8'), ('Si', '<i8'), ('Ni', '<i8')])

Spaghetti plots

In [4]:
Copied! 
simple.ccsne.plot_abundance(models, 'K-40', where='.mass==25', 
                            ax_yscale='log', ax_ylim=(1e-11,1.5e-3));
simple.ccsne.plot_abundance(models, 'K-40', where='.mass==25', ax_yscale='log', ax_ylim=(1e-11,1.5e-3));

Same plot but this time the data is plotted in moles. If the data is initially in a mass unit the abundances will be divided by the mass number of the isotope. If data is stored as mole and a mass unit is requested the adundances are instead multiplied by the mass number.

In [5]:
Copied! 
simple.ccsne.plot_abundance(models, 'Ni-60*, Ni-62*, Ni-64*', where='.mass==25 & .dataset==Ra02', unit='mol',
                            ax_xlim=(1.5, 12), ax_ylim = (1e-11,1.5e-3), ax_yscale='log', fig_size= (10,5));
simple.ccsne.plot_abundance(models, 'Ni-60*, Ni-62*, Ni-64*', where='.mass==25 & .dataset==Ra02', unit='mol', ax_xlim=(1.5, 12), ax_ylim = (1e-11,1.5e-3), ax_yscale='log', fig_size= (10,5));
INFO:SIMPLE.plot:Dividing data by the isotope mass number to convert from mass to mole
